LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ELBERT S. ACOSTA, SR. AND FELY R. ACOSTA MOTION TO EXPUNGE NOTICE OF PENDENCY OF ACTION (CCP 405.23, 405.30, 405.31 AND 405.32). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO EXPUNGE GRANTED. RULING IS NOT BASED ON TECHNICAL VIOLATION OF CCP SECTION 405.23, DEFENDANTS HAD ACTUAL NOTICE OF LIS PENDENS. IN VIEW OF THE DECLARATORY RELIEF AND INJUNCTION TO RESTORE PLAINTIFFS' POSSESSION OF THE PREMISES, THE CLAIM RELATES TO REAL PROPERTY. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT CARRIED THEIR BURDEN TO ESTABLISH BY A PREPONDERANCE OF THE EVIDENCE THE PROBABLE VALIDITY OF THEIR CLAIM TO THE PREMISES. DEFENDANTS PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT THE REASON THEY COULD NOT MAKE REPAIRS WAS BECAUSE THEY HAD NO MONEY TO DO SO, AND THEIR LISTING THE BUILDING FOR SALE SUPPORTS THIS. FURTHER, EVIDENCE IS PRESENTED THAT THE BUYERS ARE WILLING TO REPAIR THE FIRE DAMAGE AND RE-LEASE PLAINTIFFS APARTMENT TO THEM. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES DENIED, PLAINTIFFS ACTED WITH SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. CCP SECTION 405.38. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Mar-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ELBERT S. ACOSTA, SR., and, FELY R. ACOSTA Motion to Expunge Notice of Pendency of Action (CCP 405.23, 405.30, 405.31 and 405.32). Appearances: Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Attorney at Law, (415) 5330735 appearing for plaintiffs and opposing party; Gina Dashman Boer, of Haapala, Thompson & Abern LLP (510) 763-2324, appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to expunge granted. Ruling is not based on technical violation of CCP section 405.23, defendants had actual notice of lis pendens. In view of the declaratory relief and injunction to restore plaintiffs' possession of the premises, the claim relates to real property. Plaintiffs have not carried their burden to establish by a preponderance of the evidence the probable validity of their claim to the premises. Defendants present evidence that the reason they could not make repairs was because they had no money to do so, and their listing the building for sale supports this. Further, evidence is presented that the buyers are willing to repair the fire damage and re-lease plaintiffs apartment to them. Request for attorney fees denied, plaintiffs acted with substantial justification. CCP section 405.38. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
